sections.title: Total Realized Revenue
sections.content: This snapshot captures all succeeded payments within the reporting window, giving a ground-truth view of cash actually collected. Comparing total revenue against the prior period and against membership vs. one-time sources reveals whether the studio's recurring income base is holding firm. A healthy studio typically sees 60–70%+ of revenue from recurring memberships — any shortfall signals a retention or pricing opportunity.
sections.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT payment_id) AS total_payments, ROUND(SUM(amount_dollars), 2) AS total_revenue_dollars, ROUND(AVG(amount_dollars), 2) AS avg_payment_dollars, MAX(amount_dollars) AS largest_payment_dollars FROM vw_revenue WHERE status = 'succeeded' AND strftime('%Y-%m-%d', created_at) BETWEEN '2026-04-20' AND '2026-05-20'

sections.title: Revenue by Source
sections.content: The donut chart breaks realized revenue into its three primary channels — memberships, bookings, and products — for the reporting period. A membership-heavy split is a sign of predictable, recurring cash flow, while a large booking share may indicate the studio is relying on drop-ins or class packs, which are less stable. If products represent a meaningful slice, it's worth evaluating retail pricing and upsell timing to maximize that channel.
sections.sql: SELECT purchasable_type AS source, ROUND(SUM(amount_dollars), 2) AS revenue FROM vw_revenue WHERE status = 'succeeded' AND strftime('%Y-%m-%d', created_at) BETWEEN '2026-04-20' AND '2026-05-20' GROUP BY purchasable_type ORDER BY revenue DESC

sections.title: Daily Revenue Trends
sections.content: Daily revenue fluctuations expose the studio's booking and payment rhythm — spikes often align with class-pack purchases, membership renewals, or promotional pushes, while valleys can indicate low-engagement days worth targeting with outreach. A steadily rising trend line is a positive sign; a flat or declining one warrants investigation into churn, class cancellations, or slow new-member conversion. Use this view to time promotional campaigns on historically low-revenue days.
sections.sql: SELECT strftime('%Y-%m-%d', created_at) AS day, ROUND(SUM(amount_dollars), 2) AS revenue FROM vw_revenue WHERE status = 'succeeded' AND strftime('%Y-%m-%d', created_at) BETWEEN '2026-04-20' AND '2026-05-20' GROUP BY day ORDER BY day

sections.title: Revenue by Membership Plan
sections.content: Drilling into membership revenue by plan name reveals which tiers are driving the most income and whether high-volume, lower-priced plans (like the Intro Offer) are cannibalizing more profitable unlimited or founding memberships. If the Intro Offer dominates, it may indicate strong top-of-funnel conversion but weak upsell to full-price plans. Prioritizing conversion campaigns from Intro to Unlimited or Founding plans could significantly improve per-member revenue.
sections.sql: SELECT item_name AS plan_name, COUNT(DISTINCT payment_id) AS payments, ROUND(SUM(amount_dollars), 2) AS revenue FROM vw_revenue WHERE status = 'succeeded' AND purchasable_type = 'membership' AND strftime('%Y-%m-%d', created_at) BETWEEN '2026-04-20' AND '2026-05-20' GROUP BY item_name ORDER BY revenue DESC

sections.title: Recommendations
sections.content: 1. **Convert Intro Offer members:** With 550 active Intro Offer memberships dominating the base, a structured upsell sequence (automated email at day 20–25 of the intro period) nudging members toward Unlimited or Founding plans is the single highest-leverage revenue action available. 2. **Plug revenue valleys:** Use the daily trend chart to identify low-revenue days and schedule targeted flash promotions, class-pack deals, or referral incentives specifically on those days. 3. **Grow recurring base:** If bookings or one-time payments represent a large share of the donut, launch a membership conversion campaign — a limited-time discount on the first month of an Unlimited plan can shift irregular payers to predictable recurring revenue. 4. **Monitor churn against renewals:** With 56 cancelled memberships on record, track next_renewal_at dates proactively and deploy a win-back email 5 days before a likely lapse to reduce involuntary churn.
